tasks.objective: Create deterministic Go CLI skeleton, command-result JSON envelope, exit code constants, config discovery, and unit tests.
acceptance_checks: CLI exposes stable command-result envelope shape.
Exit-code constants match docs/product/prd.md.
Go module path is github.com/Clyra-AI/lumyn and Go version is pinned to 1.26.4.
T1 has no external runtime dependencies unless explicitly justified and pinned.
Tests pass without network, sandbox credentials, or model keys.
Unknown commands fail closed with exit code 2 while still emitting the command-result JSON envelope.

runtime.dependency_policy: standard library only for T1; add pinned dependencies only when required by later tasks

tasks.scope_note: T1 includes alignment drift correction from Python placeholder to pinned Go CLI runtime before implementation continues.

runtime_pins.dependency_policy: standard library first; pinned Go modules only when a task proves need and records justification
runtime_pins.live_work_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and human approval unlocks sandbox/model/network posture
runtime_pins.provider_policy: OpenAI-compatible HTTP and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters; no model key or network in deterministic bootstrap
runtime_pins.artifact_namespace: .factory/artifacts/ for Factory evidence; product artifacts use workflows/, cassettes/, runs/, baselines/, schemas/, and examples/

slice_rationale.why_this_shape: T1 is scoped to create deterministic Go CLI skeleton, command-result JSON envelope, exit code constants, config discovery, and unit tests.
slice_rationale.vertical_slice_preference: Vertical slices are preferred; foundation-only work must justify why downstream product slices need the shared contract first.

changelog.draft_entry: Initialize Lumyn Go CLI skeleton, command-result envelope, exit-code constants, config discovery, and local validation commands.

tasks.contract_impact: Introduces the public CLI shell, command-result JSON envelope, exit-code constants, and config discovery contract.
tasks.versioning_migration_impact: Pre-1.0 MVP work may evolve contracts, but introduced schema_version values, exit codes, artifact names, and command-result fields must retain explicit migration notes before release.
architecture_constraints: Preserve Go 1.26.4 and module path github.com/Clyra-AI/lumyn unless alignment artifacts are updated first.
Keep deterministic bootstrap work free of live network calls, sandbox credentials, and model keys.
Keep command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and persisted artifacts repo-relative and schema-backed where a schema exists.
Fail closed when proof strength, redaction confidence, credential posture, stale cassette state, or declared controls are ambiguous.
Record state owner, feedback source, deletion/blast-radius impact, rollback path, and evidence refs for medium/high risk architecture changes.
tasks.adr_trigger_reason: Required because this task changes public, executable, CLI, schema, artifact, CI/review, redaction/share, live/eval, or proof semantics captured by the Lumyn plan.
tdd_first_failing_tests: Add or update the smallest failing test, fixture, or schema validation for T1 acceptance before implementation when practical.
If a red-first check is not practical, record test_first_skipped_reason in the validation report with compensating evidence.

cost_perf_impact.measurement_expectation: No measurable runtime or cost increase expected beyond normal local CLI validation.
cost_perf_impact.budget_trigger: Add explicit performance/cost evidence if implementation introduces repeated replay, live network, model calls, generated artifacts, or large fixture processing.

chaos_failure_hypothesis.hypothesis: Unknown commands, invalid config paths, and write failures must still emit command-result JSON and fail closed.
chaos_failure_hypothesis.expected_fail_closed_behavior: Return structured command-result/error evidence, do not mark acceptance green, and update validation/scope closure with the failure or blocker.

semantic_invariants: Every command path emits the stable command-result envelope, including failures.
Exit codes remain aligned with docs/product/prd.md and tests.
Validation evidence records command, exit status, and skipped-command reason when applicable.
Factory evidence and product artifacts use repo-relative paths only.
No task may claim closure for PRD scope that remains missing, blocked, or deferred without explicit evidence.
tasks.plan_drift_policy: If this task changes runtime, distribution, provider, dependency, artifact namespace, live-work, guide, or validation-contract decisions, update context brief, execution plan, task packets, and validation contract before implementation continues.

engineering_policy_refs.rule: Behavior, flags, output shape, exit codes, artifact paths, install paths, and workflow semantics update docs and Factory planning artifacts together.
Command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and product artifacts are public or executable contracts.
Release-sensitive work defines version, changelog, install, artifact-integrity, and UAT evidence before merge.
Validation, work-proof, PR lifecycle, scanner, and scope-closure evidence use repo-relative refs.

architecture_guidance_refs.rule: Medium/high risk tasks record state owner, feedback source, blast radius, source-of-truth impact, and rollback/deletion checks.
Behavior and schema/output changes use test-first evidence or a structured skipped reason.
Boundary, public contract, failure semantics, credential/network, release, or major reliability/perf changes require ADR or decision note.
Fan-out, repeated runs, or model calls declare expected cost/performance impact.
Retry, cleanup, persistence, filesystem mutation, redaction, live network, and provider code require hardening/failure-mode evidence.
Deterministic bootstrap has no network/secrets; live work requires approval and evidence.

deprecated_worker_aliases.migration_behavior: block active task packets until required_worker_chain is migrated

scope_exclusions: training shared models on private artifacts

factoryd_runtime.approval_posture: human approval required for live credentials, high-risk tasks, and merge
factoryd_runtime.credential_posture: no ambient secrets during deterministic MVP bootstrap
factoryd_runtime.network_posture: offline by default until live sandbox/model work is approved

stop_conditions: required lifecycle evidence missing without approved exception
credential or network access required before approval posture is unlocked
coverage policy evidence is missing or below threshold without an approved scoped exception

coverage_policy_refs.exception_policy: coverage misses block promotion unless the task records an approved scoped exception with owner, reason, expiry or follow-up task, and compensating validation evidence

tasks.objective: Define initial schemas for workflow contract, command result, proof strength, cassette, evidence event, and redaction config.
acceptance_checks: Schemas are versioned and test-covered.
Schema names map to required schemas in docs/product/prd.md.
All 14 required Phase 0 schema files named in docs/product/prd.md exist under schemas/.
Schema tests compile every schema with a pinned JSON Schema validator.
Representative artifacts validate for workflow contract, command result, proof strength, cassette, evidence event, redaction config, and related surfaces.
Invalid command-result output fails schema validation.
Schema validator dependency is pinned in go.mod/go.sum and justified in dev guidance.

runtime.dependency_policy: Pinned github.com/santhosh-tekuri/jsonschema/v5 v5.3.1 is allowed for executable schema validation from T2 onward.

runtime_pins.provider_policy: OpenAI-compatible HTTP and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters; no model key or network in deterministic bootstrap
runtime_pins.artifact_namespace: .factory/artifacts/ for Factory evidence; product artifacts use workflows/, cassettes/, runs/, baselines/, schemas/, and examples/

slice_rationale.vertical_slice_preference: Vertical slices are preferred; foundation-only work must justify why downstream product slices need the shared contract first.

tasks.non_vertical_justification: Foundation-only work is allowed here because later product tasks depend on this repo-level contract, validation, or governance baseline before implementation can proceed safely.

acceptance_checks: .github/workflows/validate.yml exists and runs make prepush-full on pull_request and pushes to main.
Makefile lint-fast verifies the validate workflow uses Go 1.26.4 and make prepush-full.
WORKFLOW.md, AGENTS.md, README.md, docs/dev/dev_guides.md, and docs/architecture/architecture_guides.md document CI/status, review, commit-push, post-merge, and PR lifecycle evidence.
T3 and later product implementation depend on the lifecycle baseline.
The later product-facing T9 CI/JUnit/PR-comment feature remains separate from this delivery baseline.

T3 and later product task packets carry ci_lane_refs, engineering_policy_refs, architecture_guidance_refs, test_matrix_refs, and security_scanner_gates.

Exit codes remain aligned with docs/product/prd.md and tests.
Validation evidence records command, exit status, and skipped-command reason when applicable.
Factory evidence and product artifacts use repo-relative paths only.
No task may claim closure for PRD scope that remains missing, blocked, or deferred without explicit evidence.
PR/CI surfaces must distinguish skipped, stale, weak, and strong proof states.
tasks.plan_drift_policy: If this task changes runtime, distribution, provider, dependency, artifact namespace, live-work, guide, or validation-contract decisions, update context brief, execution plan, task packets, and validation contract before implementation continues.

deprecated_worker_aliases.migration_behavior: block active task packets until required_worker_chain is migrated

scope_exclusions: training shared models on private artifacts

factoryd_runtime.approval_posture: human approval required for live credentials, high-risk tasks, and merge
factoryd_runtime.credential_posture: no ambient secrets during deterministic MVP bootstrap
factoryd_runtime.network_posture: offline by default until live sandbox/model work is approved

stop_conditions: required lifecycle evidence missing without approved exception
credential or network access required before approval posture is unlocked
coverage policy evidence is missing or below threshold without an approved scoped exception

coverage_policy_refs.exception_policy: coverage misses block promotion unless the task records an approved scoped exception with owner, reason, expiry or follow-up task, and compensating validation evidence

tasks.objective: Implement `lumyn share` with redaction re-check and fail-closed export.
acceptance_checks: Share re-runs redaction before export.
Share fails closed when any value cannot be confidently redacted.

architecture_guidance_refs.rule: Medium/high risk tasks record state owner, feedback source, blast radius, source-of-truth impact, and rollback/deletion checks.
Behavior and schema/output changes use test-first evidence or a structured skipped reason.
Boundary, public contract, failure semantics, credential/network, release, or major reliability/perf changes require ADR or decision note.
Fan-out, repeated runs, or model calls declare expected cost/performance impact.
Retry, cleanup, persistence, filesystem mutation, redaction, live network, and provider code require hardening/failure-mode evidence.
Deterministic bootstrap has no network/secrets; live work requires approval and evidence.

engineering_policy_refs.rule: Behavior, flags, output shape, exit codes, artifact paths, install paths, and workflow semantics update docs and Factory planning artifacts together.
Command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and product artifacts are public or executable contracts.
Release-sensitive work defines version, changelog, install, artifact-integrity, and UAT evidence before merge.
Validation, work-proof, PR lifecycle, scanner, and scope-closure evidence use repo-relative refs.

runtime_pins.dependency_policy: standard library first; pinned Go modules only when a task proves need and records justification
runtime_pins.live_work_policy: blocked until deterministic replay foundation passes and human approval unlocks sandbox/model/network posture
runtime_pins.provider_policy: OpenAI-compatible HTTP and Anthropic Messages HTTP adapters; no model key or network in deterministic bootstrap
runtime_pins.artifact_namespace: .factory/artifacts/ for Factory evidence; product artifacts use workflows/, cassettes/, runs/, baselines/, schemas/, and examples/

slice_rationale.why_this_shape: T10 is scoped to implement `lumyn share` with redaction re-check and fail-closed export.
slice_rationale.vertical_slice_preference: Vertical slices are preferred; foundation-only work must justify why downstream product slices need the shared contract first.

changelog.draft_entry: Add `lumyn share` with redaction re-check and fail-closed export.

tasks.contract_impact: Adds `lumyn share` export behavior and redaction re-check/fail-closed contracts.
tasks.versioning_migration_impact: Pre-1.0 MVP work may evolve contracts, but introduced schema_version values, exit codes, artifact names, and command-result fields must retain explicit migration notes before release.
architecture_constraints: Preserve Go 1.26.4 and module path github.com/Clyra-AI/lumyn unless alignment artifacts are updated first.
Keep deterministic bootstrap work free of live network calls, sandbox credentials, and model keys.
Keep command-result JSON, exit codes, schemas, and persisted artifacts repo-relative and schema-backed where a schema exists.
Fail closed when proof strength, redaction confidence, credential posture, stale cassette state, or declared controls are ambiguous.
Record state owner, feedback source, deletion/blast-radius impact, rollback path, and evidence refs for medium/high risk architecture changes.
Redaction uncertainty blocks persistence or export and must surface structured failure evidence.
tasks.adr_trigger_reason: Required because this task changes public, executable, CLI, schema, artifact, CI/review, redaction/share, live/eval, or proof semantics captured by the Lumyn plan.
tdd_first_failing_tests: Add or update the smallest failing test, fixture, or schema validation for T10 acceptance before implementation when practical.
If a red-first check is not practical, record test_first_skipped_reason in the validation report with compensating evidence.

cost_perf_impact.measurement_expectation: Keep deterministic commands fast and local; live/eval work must record duration, retry, and cost evidence before closure.
cost_perf_impact.budget_trigger: Add explicit performance/cost evidence if implementation introduces repeated replay, live network, model calls, generated artifacts, or large fixture processing.

chaos_failure_hypothesis.hypothesis: Any value that cannot be confidently redacted must stop share export before writing a shareable bundle.
chaos_failure_hypothesis.expected_fail_closed_behavior: Return structured command-result/error evidence, do not mark acceptance green, and update validation/scope closure with the failure or blocker.

semantic_invariants: Every command path emits the stable command-result envelope, including failures.
Exit codes remain aligned with docs/product/prd.md and tests.
Validation evidence records command, exit status, and skipped-command reason when applicable.
Factory evidence and product artifacts use repo-relative paths only.
No task may claim closure for PRD scope that remains missing, blocked, or deferred without explicit evidence.
Schema-backed artifacts must validate against the declared schema version or fail closure.

Eval does not become the default hard CI gate.
Eval runs OpenAI-compatible and Anthropic provider/model adapters when configured.
Provider config records provider, model, temperature, base_url, and api_key_env.
